compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · CONTAINER · [G4] Child consistency — pairing (parent 6f2d ⟶ child 4024 WS-DOC), cross-checked against the complete authoritative 2-child roster [WS-DOC (4024) + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (b4af)] — VERDICT: 1 finding (ordering/sequencing).

Scope note: per the latest SCOPE-DECISION comment on 6f2d, the authoritative residual scope narrowed to WS-DOC + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (WS-VISUAL-EVAL split off into its own epic; WS-MIGRATE/WS-PROMPTS/WS-CLEANUP/WS-VISUAL/WS-VALIDATE/WS-INTENT-ALIGN all DONE via closed sibling epics 5766/72d5/e6d9/681b). So the 7-mode cross-child check runs WS-DOC against exactly one live sibling: WS-EVAL-EXISTING (b4af).

7-mode audit (CLEAN except ordering):
- Implicit shared state — CLEAN. Both children touch the eval seam, but the division is build (b4af) vs document (4024); WS-DOC reads what WS-EVAL-EXISTING produces ("reflects WS-EVAL-EXISTING as built"). No undeclared shared mutable artifact.
- Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN. The two-tier eval model is described identically in both: deterministic gate on every PR (zero-cost) + LLM-judge tier OFF the PR critical path (manual + weekly schedule, non-blocking, cached, concurrency-capped, fork-safe). WS-DOC AC4 and WS-EVAL-EXISTING AC4/AC5 agree on tiers, triggers, and rails — no contradiction.
- Scope overlap — CLEAN. Documenting the eval seam (4024) vs building it + finishing the run machinery (b4af) is a clean labor split, not duplication.
- Dependency gap — CLEAN (no missing producer). The doc's content dependency on the eval seam has a real producer in-roster (b4af); the dependency TARGET is not absent — see ordering finding for the un-declared sequencing.
- Consumer impact — CLEAN / deferred. Per the epic's own agent-vs-single-turn bright line, G4 defers code-grounded consumer-impact to E4/G1G2/A1; no plan-level consumer breakage between the two siblings.
- Residual references — CLEAN. WS-DOC names WS-EVAL-EXISTING accurately; no stale references to descoped/relocated workstreams (WS-VISUAL-EVAL/WS-MIGRATE et al.) as if they were live siblings of this epic.
- Ordering violation — FINDING (below).

FINDING (G4 · ordering):

- [ ] WS-DOC must document the eval seam "as built" by WS-EVAL-EXISTING, but no dependency/ordering link sequences the two — WS-DOC could be authored before the seam it must mirror is finished.
  - criteria: [G4]
  - location: child 4024 WS-DOC — Scope bullet "...how the live tier is triggered + cost/fork-safety rails (reflects WS-EVAL-EXISTING as built)" and AC4 "Documents the eval seam: two-tier model, how to author a spec, how the live tier is triggered, cost + fork-safety rails (consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING)"; sibling b4af WS-EVAL-EXISTING AC4/AC5 (the eval-discipline + eval-live tiers being BUILT).
  - evidence:
    - WS-DOC explicitly binds its eval-seam documentation to WS-EVAL-EXISTING's delivered state: "reflects WS-EVAL-EXISTING as built" and AC4 "(consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING)".
    - The 6f2d RE-TRIAGE/SCOPE comments confirm the eval LIVE tier is presently an unwired stub (run_eval() raises NotImplementedError; deterministic scorer names are inert; eval-live step is a placeholder echo) — i.e. the "as built" target does NOT yet exist; b4af is the ticket that builds it (Phase 2.1–2.5: scorer registry, live solver, datasets, fixtures, eval-live CI wiring + the cost/fork-safety rails).
    - ABSENCE confirmed against the whole roster: neither 4024 nor b4af carries a deps entry (both show "deps": []); there is no depends_on / blocked-by linking WS-DOC's authoring to WS-EVAL-EXISTING's completion. So nothing sequences "document the seam" after "build the seam."
  - scenarios:
    - WS-DOC is authored first (both are open, priority 2, no deps); the cost/fork-safety rails and live-tier trigger mechanics are still in flux (or still stubbed) in b4af, so the doc's AC4 ("how the live tier is triggered, cost + fork-safety rails") documents an intended-but-unbuilt design that later drifts from b4af's actual eval-live CI wiring — re-introducing exactly the "knowledge scattered / doc out of date" problem WS-DOC exists to fix.
    - Conversely, if b4af lands after the doc, WS-DOC's "consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING" AC cannot be truthfully checked at WS-DOC close time.
  - impact: Low-to-moderate sequencing risk. Not a contradiction (the two descriptions agree today), but the cross-child binding is asserted ("as built", "consistent with") without an ordering link to enforce it, so the doc can be completed against a moving/unbuilt target and silently drift.
  - checklist_item: Check the 7 cross-child modes — ordering violation.
  - suggested_fix: Add an explicit ordering link so the eval-seam SECTION of WS-DOC follows WS-EVAL-EXISTING's eval-live delivery — e.g. a depends_on (or blocked-by) from 4024 → b4af scoped to the eval-seam AC, OR split WS-DOC's eval-seam documentation to land in the same change as / immediately after b4af's eval-live CI wiring. If the team intends WS-DOC to be authored first as a design anchor, state that direction explicitly (WS-EVAL-EXISTING implements the seam WS-DOC specifies) and make b4af responsible for reconciling the doc to the as-built rails at its close, so the "consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING" AC has a defined enforcement point.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · CONTAINER · [G4] Child consistency — pairing (parent 6f2d ⟶ child 4024 WS-DOC), cross-checked against the complete authoritative 2-child roster [WS-DOC (4024) + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (b4af)] — VERDICT: 1 finding (ordering/sequencing).

Scope note: per the authoritative SCOPE-DECISION on 6f2d (and the epic body's "REMAINING (this epic's only live deliverables)" line), the live residual scope is exactly WS-DOC + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (WS-VISUAL-EVAL split into its own epic yip-gem-teak/7013; WS-MIGRATE/WS-PROMPTS/WS-CLEANUP/WS-VISUAL/WS-VALIDATE/WS-INTENT-ALIGN all DONE via closed sibling epics 5766/72d5/e6d9/681b). So the 7-mode cross-child check runs WS-DOC against exactly one live sibling: WS-EVAL-EXISTING (b4af).

7-mode audit (CLEAN except ordering):
- Implicit shared state — CLEAN. Both children touch the eval seam, but the division is BUILD (b4af) vs DOCUMENT (4024); WS-DOC reads what WS-EVAL-EXISTING produces ("reflects WS-EVAL-EXISTING as built"). No undeclared shared mutable artifact; b4af does not silently depend on anything 4024 mutates.
- Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN. The two-tier eval model is described identically on both sides: deterministic gate on every PR at zero API cost + LLM-judge tier OFF the PR critical path (workflow_dispatch + weekly schedule, non-blocking/continue-on-error, response-cached, concurrency-capped, fork-safe, spend-cap-guided). WS-DOC AC4 and WS-EVAL-EXISTING AC4/AC5 agree on tiers, triggers, and rails — no contradiction.
- Scope overlap — CLEAN. Documenting the eval seam (4024) vs building it + finishing the run machinery (b4af) is a clean labor split, not duplication. b4af claims no documentation deliverable; 4024 claims no build deliverable.
- Dependency gap — CLEAN (no missing producer). The doc's content dependency on the eval seam has a real producer in-roster (b4af); the dependency TARGET is not absent. The un-declared sequencing is captured as the ordering finding below.
- Consumer impact — CLEAN / deferred. Per the epic's own agent-vs-single-turn bright line, G4 defers code-grounded consumer-impact to E4/G1G2/A1; no plan-level consumer breakage between the two siblings.
- Residual references — CLEAN. WS-DOC names WS-EVAL-EXISTING accurately; no stale references to descoped/relocated workstreams (WS-VISUAL-EVAL / WS-MIGRATE et al.) as if they were live siblings of this epic.
- Ordering violation — FINDING (below).

FINDING (G4 · ordering violation):

- finding: WS-DOC must document the eval seam "as built" by WS-EVAL-EXISTING, but no dependency/ordering link sequences the two — WS-DOC could be authored (and closed) before the seam it must mirror is finished, so the doc can be written against a moving/unbuilt target and silently drift.
  - criteria: [G4]
  - location: child 4024 WS-DOC — Scope bullet "...how the live tier is triggered + cost/fork-safety rails (reflects WS-EVAL-EXISTING as built)" and AC4 "Documents the eval seam: two-tier model, how to author a spec, how the live tier is triggered, cost + fork-safety rails (consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING)"; sibling b4af WS-EVAL-EXISTING AC3/AC4/AC5 (run_eval() live tier, eval-discipline + eval-live tiers being BUILT, fork-safety/spend-cap rails).
  - evidence:
    - WS-DOC explicitly binds its eval-seam documentation to WS-EVAL-EXISTING's delivered state: "reflects WS-EVAL-EXISTING as built" (Scope) and AC4 "(consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING)".
    - The "as built" target does NOT yet exist: per the 6f2d SCOPE-DECISION comment and b4af's "Why", the live tier is presently an unwired stub — run_eval() raises NotImplementedError, deterministic scorer names are inert strings with no registry, and the eval-live step is a placeholder echo. b4af is the ticket that builds it (Phase 2.1–2.5: scorer registry, live solver + context-injection, datasets, fixtures, eval-live CI wiring + cost/fork-safety rails).
    - ABSENCE confirmed against the whole roster: neither 4024 nor b4af carries a deps entry (both show "deps": []); there is no depends_on / blocked-by linking WS-DOC's authoring to WS-EVAL-EXISTING's completion. So nothing sequences "document the seam" after "build the seam."
  - scenarios:
    - Both children are open, priority 2, no deps. WS-DOC is authored first; the cost/fork-safety rails and live-tier trigger mechanics are still in flux (or still stubbed) in b4af, so WS-DOC AC4 ("how the live tier is triggered, cost + fork-safety rails") documents an intended-but-unbuilt design that later drifts from b4af's actual eval-live CI wiring — re-introducing exactly the "knowledge scattered / doc out of date" problem WS-DOC exists to fix.
    - Conversely, if b4af lands after WS-DOC closes, WS-DOC's "consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING" AC cannot be truthfully verified at WS-DOC close time (the comparand does not yet exist).
  - impact: Low-to-moderate sequencing risk. Not a contradiction (the two descriptions agree today), but the cross-child binding is asserted ("as built", "consistent with") with no ordering link to enforce it, so the doc's eval-seam section can be completed against a moving/unbuilt target and silently drift, defeating the doc's own purpose.
  - checklist_item: Check the 7 cross-child modes — ordering violation.
  - suggested_fix: Add an explicit ordering link so the eval-seam SECTION of WS-DOC follows WS-EVAL-EXISTING's eval-live delivery — e.g. a depends_on (or blocked-by) from 4024 → b4af scoped to the eval-seam AC, OR split WS-DOC's eval-seam documentation to land in the same change as / immediately after b4af's eval-live CI wiring. If the team intends WS-DOC to be authored first as a design anchor, state that direction explicitly (WS-EVAL-EXISTING implements the seam WS-DOC specifies) and make b4af responsible for reconciling the doc to the as-built rails at its close, so the "consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING" AC has a defined enforcement point.

(Note: an identical ordering finding was previously recorded on this pairing at timestamp 1782579130344411001; this re-affirms it after the full 7-mode re-check. The mirror b4af-side pairing correctly treats b4af as the producer carrying no independent ordering obligation, so the interaction is owned here and not double-counted there.)

WS-DOC authored first; cost/fork-safety rails + live-tier trigger mechanics still in flux/stubbed in b4af, so WS-DOC AC4 documents an intended-but-unbuilt design that later drifts from b4af's actual eval-live CI wiring — re-introducing exactly the "doc out of date" problem WS-DOC exists to fix.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: docs/workflow-engine.md committed (commit 43283d6e) and linked from README + CLAUDE.md doc index; two-audience split (client run/use vs rebar-dev author/edit), grounded in the real workflow CLI + DSL + the eval seam built in WS-EVAL-EXISTING. Closing.

compiled_state.description:
# WS-DOC — docs/workflow-engine.md (intended use, for two audiences)

## Why

The workflow engine (epic a88f, closed; rebuilt under e6d9) has no canonical project
doc describing its INTENDED USE. Knowledge is scattered across README, docs/llm-framework.md,
CLAUDE.md, and closed tickets. New users and maintainers have no single anchor.

## What

Author `docs/workflow-engine.md` as the canonical anchor the review epics (5fd2/9da1)
point at. It MUST serve two distinct audiences and clearly separate them:
- a CLIENT / operator who will mostly RUN workflows and gates, not edit them;
- a REBAR DEV who authors/edits workflows, prompts, criteria, and evals.

## Scope

- When to use a workflow vs a bespoke op; the YAML DSL + schema; author/validate/render/run.
- Scripted vs agentic steps; the agent-vs-single-turn bright line.
- The three-tier (three-pass) LLM-review gating pattern (evidence -> binary-verify -> deterministic gate).
- The prompt-library + eval seam, including the two-tier eval model (deterministic gate every PR;
  LLM-judge tier off the PR critical path) and how the live tier is triggered + cost/fork-safety rails
  (reflects WS-EVAL-EXISTING as built).
- Prompt-authoring guidance for reviewer criteria (the research-backed do/don't list from the epic body).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/workflow-engine.md` exists and is linked from README + CLAUDE.md doc index.
- [ ] A clear CLIENT-vs-DEV split: a client can find run/use guidance without wading through authoring internals; a dev finds the authoring + eval surface.
- [ ] Documents the YAML DSL, scripted vs agentic steps, the agent-vs-single-turn bright line, and the three-pass gating pattern.
- [ ] Documents the eval seam: two-tier model, how to author a spec, how the live tier is triggered, cost + fork-safety rails (consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING).
- [ ] No contradiction with docs/llm-framework.md / docs/plan-review-gate.md (cross-link, don't duplicate).

file_impact.reason: new canonical workflow-engine usage doc (client vs dev audiences)
add docs/workflow-engine.md to the doc index

compiled_state.title: WS-DOC: author docs/workflow-engine.md (client vs dev audiences)
